title: Guide for considering influenza testing when influenza viruses are circulating in the community | Influenza (Flu) | CDC
text:

Influenza testing may help inform decisions on infection prevention and control practices. 
Persons who are at Higher Risk of Complications from Influenza include those aged ≥65 years or <2 years; pregnant women; persons with chronic lung disease (including asthma), heart disease, renal, metabolic, hematologic and neurologic disease; immunosuppression; morbid obesity; some racial and ethnic minority groups; and residents of chronic care facilities. 
Antiviral treatment is recommended as soon as possible for outpatients with suspected or confirmed influenza who are at high risk for complications from influenza, or those with progressive disease not requiring hospital admission. Outpatients who are not at higher risk of complications from influenza illness can be considered for antiviral treatment based upon clinical judgment if they present within 2 days of illness onset. Guidance on antiviral treatment of influenza is available . 
Dec. 19, 2025
